programme.title: Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time
programme.desc: St Bartholomew's Hospital is one of the most extraordinary institutions in the world: the oldest hospital in Britain still operating on its original site, and today a global leader in cutting-edge medicine. This series has unprecedented access to both its modern wards and its private archive - a unique collection spanning nearly 1,000 years of medical history, unseen anywhere else. Leading the series is Professor Alice Roberts. From brutal early surgery to robot-assisted operations, she reveals how centuries of risk and innovation shaped modern medicine. In this episode she heads to Barts' extraordinary archive, which holds almost 900 years of fascinating and priceless artefacts; we follow the team as they spring into action for an urgent cardiac emergency; explore the grisly history of x-rays; and visit The Old Operating Theatre where a 1600s manual, The Surgeon's Mate, prompts a re-enactment of the gruesome amputation of a poor woman's gangrenous limbs. Exec Prod: Sarah Sarkhel; Series Prod: Giulia Clark; Lion TV (Ep1)

programme.title: The Surgeon
programme.desc: For the first time, this series reveals exactly what life is really like as a surgeon: from the high pressure world of the operating theatre to the consulting room where news must be broken (both good and bad), to the everyday ups and downs of life at home. Each episode follows a different surgeon as they juggle these very different worlds, making life-changing decisions on a daily basis. It's a dramatic and deeply personal series. In this first episode we follow Daren Francis, one of the UK's leading bowel cancer surgeons, who works across three different hospitals in north London. Among his patients are retired nurse Doris, who is in great pain with a tumour that he suspects is cancerous and blocking her bowel; and 67-year-old Antonio, to whom Daren must break the news that he has a lump in his bowel which is cancer. Series Prod: Freddie Foss-Smith; Exec Prod: David Granger; Dirs: Kat Bannon/Anna Davis; idle Industries (Ep1/4)
